title: myrcia decandra
intro: Myrcia decandra, also known as the 'Santa Maria' or 'Golden Shower', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a shrub or small tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, Myrcia decandra is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
